# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

TEMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
TEMP_RESULT="/tmp/myapp-$$-result.tmp"

# Write initial data to temp file
echo "Processing data batch 1" > "$TEMP_FILE"
for i in {1..10}; do
    echo "Record $i: $(date +%s)" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
done

# Process the data
cat "$TEMP_FILE" | grep "Record" | awk '{print $2, $3}' > "$TEMP_RESULT"

# Append more data to same temp file
echo "Processing data batch 2" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
for i in {11..20}; do
    echo "Record $i: $(date +%s)" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
done

# Process additional data
cat "$TEMP_FILE" | grep "Record" | awk '{print $2, $3}' >> "$TEMP_RESULT"

# Read and display results
echo "Final Results:"
cat "$TEMP_RESULT"

# Count processed records
RECORD_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_RESULT")
echo "Total records processed: $RECORD_COUNT"

# Store summary in original temp file
echo "Summary: $RECORD_COUNT records processed at $(date)" > "$TEMP_FILE"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"

# Cleanup
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE" "$TEMP_RESULT"
